core.action: Validate transition matrix time-homogeneity using statistical tests (e.g., Chi-square test for stationarity across time windows) and document whether homogeneity assumption holds

consequence.description: Without time-homogeneity validation, non-stationary transition matrices produce incorrect probability estimates, causing Markov chain models to fail when market regimes shift

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-GAP-006

core.when: When implementing iterative models (EM algorithm, Baum-Welch, gradient descent optimization)
core.action: Assume models converge without explicit convergence monitoring — iterative models may converge to wrong solutions or fail to converge

consequence.description: Without explicit convergence parameters (tolerance, max_iter, convergence check), iterative models may run indefinitely or stop prematurely, producing unreliable parameter estimates

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-GAP-010

core.when: When implementing iterative optimization models
core.action: Add explicit convergence parameters: tolerance (e.g., 1e-6), max_iter (e.g., 1000), and convergence_status flag; implement early stopping with logging when convergence fails

consequence.description: Missing convergence parameters causes silent non-convergence or infinite loops in production, leading to timeout failures or inaccurate model parameters without warning

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-GAP-010

validation_threshold: tolerance parameter missing OR max_iter parameter missing → FAIL

core.when: When creating TimeSeries objects with irregular time intervals
core.action: Provide explicit freq parameter when TimeSeries has irregular time intervals — automatic frequency inference will fail or produce incorrect frequency for irregular series

consequence.description: Without explicit freq parameter for irregular series, frequency-based operations (resampling, lagged features) produce misaligned data or incorrect results, breaking time-dependent strategy logic

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-057

core.when: When implementing or refactoring TimeSeries static covariate handling
core.action: Store static covariates as pd.DataFrame with categorical dtype — do not replace with dict, separate arrays, or other structures; models expecting DataFrame access will break

consequence.description: Replacing DataFrame storage with dict or separate arrays breaks models relying on DataFrame operations for static covariates, causing AttributeError or incorrect covariate access in hierarchical models

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-060

core.when: When implementing or refactoring TimeSeries hierarchy structure
core.action: Store hierarchy as dict mapping component names to parent aggregations — do not replace with nested structures, lists, or other formats; reconciliation transformers expect dict O(1) lookup

consequence.description: Replacing dict hierarchy with nested or flat structures breaks O(1) hierarchy lookup in reconciliation, causing performance degradation and incorrect bottom-up/top-down aggregation in hierarchical models

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-061

core.when: When implementing or modifying data transformation operations in backtesting
core.action: Change the copy=False parameter in transformer internal operations — transformers must maintain the copy=False contract to verify time series immutability and external reference validity

consequence.description: Changing copy=False to copy=True doubles memory usage for large time series operations, causing potential out-of-memory errors and significantly degrading backtest performance for high-frequency or large datasets

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-082

core.when: When implementing hierarchical forecast reconciliation logic
core.action: Switch the reconciliator from BottomUp aggregation to TopDown or Optimal methods — BottomUp ensures parent forecasts equal the mathematical sum of children forecasts, maintaining hierarchical consistency

consequence.description: Using TopDown or Optimal reconciliators breaks the fundamental hierarchical consistency property where parent = sum(children), causing forecast aggregation errors that compound through the hierarchy

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-083

core.when: When processing time series with missing values for model training
core.action: Remove the min_gap_size parameter or hardcode a fixed threshold in extract_subseries — min_gap_size must remain configurable to properly split discontinuous segments and prevent gap corruption of continuous models

consequence.description: Hardcoding or removing min_gap_size either fragments valid continuous series with too-small thresholds or corrupts models with discontinuous data when threshold is too large, degrading forecast accuracy

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-087

core.when: When implementing or configuring DTW alignment with Itakura parallelogram window
core.action: Remove the max_slope parameter or hardcode it to extreme values in Itakura parallelogram — max_slope controls slope deviation from 45 degrees and must remain tunable to prevent excessive compression or expansion in alignment

consequence.description: Removing slope constraints or using extreme values allows alignment compression/expansion extremes, causing misaligned time series that distort similarity measurements and degrade pattern matching accuracy

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-092

core.when: When implementing multivariate anomaly detection aggregation
core.action: Replace OR logic with AND logic or voting mechanisms in the anomaly score aggregator — OR logic ensures maximum sensitivity (recall) by flagging anomalies when any detector identifies them, which is critical when missing anomalies is costly

consequence.description: Switching to AND or voting reduces anomaly detection recall, causing true anomalies to be missed when only one detector flags them, leading to undetected risk events and potential financial losses in live trading

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-096

core.when: When implementing or refactoring ConformalPrediction calibration logic
core.action: Use calibration_series parameter to compute nonconformity scores on held-out data — do not use training or test data for calibration to prevent information leakage

consequence.description: Without proper calibration_series separation, prediction intervals achieve incorrect coverage, causing systematic under or over-confidence in forecast uncertainty estimates and potentially poor risk management decisions

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-100

core.when: When implementing metric computation functions for time series evaluation
core.action: Support both deterministic (single-value) and stochastic (multi-sample) TimeSeries inputs — apply sample aggregation for stochastic series to produce unified metric outputs

consequence.description: Dropping stochastic series support breaks evaluation for probabilistic forecasting models, causing metric computation failures when comparing probabilistic and point forecast approaches

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-101

core.when: When implementing or modifying MAPE metric calculation in the evaluation module
core.action: Use symmetric MAPE formula: 2*|actual-pred|/(|actual|+|pred|) — maintain this exact formulation to handle near-zero values without division errors and verify fair penalization of over and under-predictions

consequence.description: Switching to asymmetric standard MAPE causes division errors near zero and biased model selection that systematically favors over-predicting models, leading to suboptimal trading strategy choices

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-102

core.when: When implementing dataset loading and caching infrastructure
core.action: Include DatasetLoaderMetadata with uri, hash, and format specifications for each dataset — use hash for cache validation and format for parsing configuration

consequence.description: Without metadata structure, dataset caching becomes unreliable and reproducibility is lost; repeated downloads occur for unchanged datasets and data integrity cannot be verified

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-105

core.when: When defining or modifying monthly time series dataset specifications
core.action: Use header_time='Month' with format_time='%Y-%m' for monthly datasets — this ISO-style format ensures unambiguous parsing independent of locale settings

consequence.description: Using locale-specific or inconsistent date formats causes monthly dataset parsing failures or incorrect date interpretation on systems with different regional settings

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-107

core.when: When implementing or replacing distance calculations in time series comparison
core.action: Maintain L1 norm (absolute difference) as the default distance metric for univariate series and sum of L1 for multivariate series — verify outlier robustness is preserved in any refactoring

consequence.description: Switching to L2 (Euclidean) distance without explicit consideration introduces quadratic sensitivity to outliers, causing distance-based methods to over-weight extreme values and produce incorrect similarity measurements for noisy financial time series

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-123

core.when: When processing static covariates before feeding into forecasting models
core.action: Use MinMaxScaler for numerical static covariates and OrdinalEncoder for categorical static covariates — do not apply one-hot encoding without explicit justification as it causes dimensionality explosion for high-cardinality categorical features

consequence.description: One-hot encoding categorical static covariates with high cardinality (e.g., industry codes, country identifiers) dramatically increases feature dimensionality, causing memory overflow and training degradation in large-scale backtesting scenarios

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-131

core.when: When implementing monetary calculations and data storage in backtesting
core.action: Assume the framework annotates currency and unit for each monetary values implicitly — currency/unit metadata is absent and must be explicitly provided

consequence.description: Without explicit currency/unit annotations, cross-currency calculations produce incorrect results (e.g., USD and CNY amounts added together), leading to systematic mispricing in portfolio valuations that accumulate silently

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-GAP-003

core.when: When implementing monetary calculations or data storage
core.action: Add explicit currency field (e.g., 'currency': 'CNY' or 'USD') and unit field to each monetary value parameters; validate matching currencies before arithmetic operations across different values

consequence.description: Cross-currency operations without validation cause portfolio P&L to be calculated incorrectly, with losses accumulating silently until discovered in audit review

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-GAP-003

core.when: When implementing market order execution logic in backtesting
core.action: Assume market orders are filled instantly at the exact quoted price without any fill assumption modeling — the framework does not implement fill assumptions for market orders

consequence.description: Treating market orders as instantaneous perfect fills ignores liquidity constraints and partial fill scenarios, causing backtests to overstate returns for large order sizes in illiquid markets

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-GAP-005

core.when: When executing market orders in backtesting
core.action: Implement fill assumption logic by checking available_volume against order_size; if order_size exceeds available_volume, apply partial fill percentage and reject or queue remaining volume; add slippage_model parameter for price impact estimation

consequence.description: Without fill assumption modeling, large market orders assume full execution at expected prices when actual fills experience partial fills or price impact, causing live P&L to diverge significantly from backtested results

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-GAP-005

core.when: When implementing multi-provider data source configuration
core.action: Assume a single credential set works uniformly across each providers without priority ranking or isolation — the framework does not implement provider priority or credential isolation

consequence.description: Using undifferentiated credentials across providers without priority ranking causes auth failures when providers have different key formats or rate limits, leading to cascading operation failures

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-GAP-007

core.when: When configuring multiple data providers
core.action: Implement provider priority configuration with ordered fallback list (e.g., primary_provider, backup_provider); each provider must have isolated credential storage with separate keys and namespaces; implement fail-over logic to next provider when primary fails authentication

consequence.description: Without provider isolation and priority ranking, credential rotation or revocation affects all providers simultaneously, causing cascading service failures across all connected data sources

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-GAP-007

core.when: When implementing trade authorization logic
core.action: Assume single-agent approval is sufficient for trade execution without multi-agent consensus or independent risk verification — the framework does not implement multi-agent consensus or risk veto

consequence.description: Single-agent authorization creates a single point of failure where a compromised or erroneous agent can execute unauthorized large trades, amplifying losses without any independent check or veto

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-GAP-008

core.when: When implementing trade authorization and risk management
core.action: Implement multi-agent consensus requiring at least 2 independent agents to approve high-value trades (define threshold, e.g., notional > $100,000); risk veto agent must independently verify position limits and exposure before execution; dissenting votes trigger alert and block execution

consequence.description: Without multi-agent consensus and risk veto, runaway or compromised agents can execute large directional trades causing catastrophic losses that independent risk review could have prevented

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-GAP-008

core.when: When implementing transaction status management
core.action: Assume the framework maintains mutable state for transaction status without append-only logging — the framework does not implement immutable transformation logs for data lineage

consequence.description: Mutable status fields without append-only logs allow transaction status to be retroactively modified, destroying audit trail integrity and enabling fraud or accidental data tampering to go undetected

evidence_refs.summary: 5d9404e Derived from BD: BD-GAP-014

core.when: When implementing transaction status tracking
core.action: Implement append-only transformation log for each status changes; each status update must create a new immutable log entry containing timestamp, previous_status, new_status, and actor_id; verify log entries are write-once and cannot be updated or deleted

consequence.description: Without immutable transformation logs, regulatory audits cannot verify transaction lineage, potentially resulting in compliance violations, failed audits, and regulatory penalties
